title: Khirachora Gopinatha Temple
tagline: Khirachora Gopinatha Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Kshirachora Gopinatha Temple (Odia: କ୍ଷୀରଚୋରା ଗୋପୀନାଥ ମନ୍ଦିର) is a Hindu temple in Remuna, Odisha, India.
overview.description: Kshirachora Gopinatha Temple (Odia: କ୍ଷୀରଚୋରା ଗୋପୀନାଥ ମନ୍ଦିର) is a Hindu temple in Remuna, Odisha, India. The name "Remuna" is from the word "Ramaniya" which means very good-looking. "Kshirachora" in Odia means Stealer of condensed Milk and Gopinatha means the Divine Consort of Gopis. The reference is to child Krishna's love for milk and milk products.

topPlaces.name: Remuna
topPlaces.description: Remuna is a town and a notified area committee in Baleswar district in the Indian state of Odisha. It is famous for the Khirachora Gopinatha Temple.

topPlaces.name: Government Polytechnic, Balasore
topPlaces.description: The Government Polytechnic, Balasore is a State Government run diploma engineering school. It is established in 2013 by the Directorate of Technical Education & Vocational Training(DTET), Orissa.

topPlaces.name: Baneswara Temple, Balasore
topPlaces.description: Baneswara Temple is a Hindu temple in Balasore, Odisha, India. It is one of the oldest in the city.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Balasore
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Balasore (Danish: Slaget ved Balasore) was an engagement between Bengali and English ships against Danish ships at Balasore. When the English failed to persuade the Danes, the Bengalis started attacking the English vessel, yet the English were rescued by the Dutch.

topPlaces.name: Balasore
topPlaces.description: Balasore, also known as Baleshwar, is a city in the state of Odisha, about 215 km (134 mi) from the state capital Bhubaneswar and 240 km (149 mi) from Kolkata, in eastern India. It is the administrative headquarters of Balasore district and the largest city as well as health and educational hub of northern Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Expulsion of Danes from Balasore
topPlaces.description: The Expulsion of Danes from Balasore (Danish: Udvisningen af danskerene fra Balasore) was a violent expulsion and ousting of the Danish East India Company from the Mughal harbour and trading hub of Balasore in 1643 or 1644 by the local Mughal governor, Malik Beg.

topPlaces.name: Bahanaga
topPlaces.description: Bahanaga, is a town in Balasore district of the Indian state of Odisha. It's one of the major business spots in the Balasore district.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Balasore
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Balasore (Latin: Balasoren(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Balasore in the ecclesiastical province of Cuttack–Bhubaneswar in India. On Monday, 9 December 2013, Pope Francis accepted the resignation from the pastoral governance of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Balasore, presented by Bishop Thomas Thiruthalil, CM, in accordance with Canon 401.1 of the Latin Rite 1983 Code of Canon Law.

items.note: Remuna is a town and a notified area committee in Baleswar district in the Indian state of Odisha. It is famous for the Khirachora Gopinatha Temple.
The Government Polytechnic, Balasore is a State Government run diploma engineering school. It is established in 2013 by the Directorate of Technical Education & Vocational Training(DTET), Orissa.
Baneswara Temple is a Hindu temple in Balasore, Odisha, India. It is one of the oldest in the city.

The Battle of Balasore (Danish: Slaget ved Balasore) was an engagement between Bengali and English ships against Danish ships at Balasore. When the English failed to persuade the Danes, the Bengalis started attacking the English vessel, yet the English were rescued by the Dutch.

Balasore, also known as Baleshwar, is a city in the state of Odisha, about 215 km (134 mi) from the state capital Bhubaneswar and 240 km (149 mi) from Kolkata, in eastern India. It is the administrative headquarters of Balasore district and the largest city as well as health and educational hub of northern Odisha.

The Expulsion of Danes from Balasore (Danish: Udvisningen af danskerene fra Balasore) was a violent expulsion and ousting of the Danish East India Company from the Mughal harbour and trading hub of Balasore in 1643 or 1644 by the local Mughal governor, Malik Beg.
Bahanaga, is a town in Balasore district of the Indian state of Odisha. It's one of the major business spots in the Balasore district.

The Roman Catholic Diocese of Balasore (Latin: Balasoren(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Balasore in the ecclesiastical province of Cuttack–Bhubaneswar in India. On Monday, 9 December 2013, Pope Francis accepted the resignation from the pastoral governance of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Balasore, presented by Bishop Thomas Thiruthalil, CM, in accordance with Canon 401.1 of the Latin Rite 1983 Code of Canon Law.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Khirachora Gopinatha Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Khirachora Gopinatha Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Khirachora Gopinatha Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (181 km) and the nearest railway station is Jamshedpur (Tatanagar) (156 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Khirachora Gopinatha Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Khirachora Gopinatha Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Khirachora Gopinatha Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Khirachora Gopinatha Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Khirachora Gopinatha Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Remuna, Government Polytechnic, Balasore, Baneswara Temple, Balasore, Battle of Balasore, Balasore.
